free_text: Back scattering of alpha-particles by C-12 and C-13.
Institute of nuclear physics, electronics and
Alpha-particle beam was focused with a pair of
quadruple lenses and was deflected by turning the
magnet to the target under study. Before this it passed
through two quadruple lenses and several collimating
Beam divergence was less than 0.02 degree.
crystals were placed under constant angles of 45-50
degrees to an incident beam to measure the beam current
Faraday cup with current integrator was used.
Carbon foils of 0.28 mg/cm2 thickness were used as a
target. They were enriched in C-13 up to 80%. The
target was placed in the center of scattering chamber
Error of a scattering angle measurement
English translation in REFERENCE was added.
